title: Agentic Workflow Design
description: Agentic Workflow Design is the process of choosing the right architecture pattern for a specific task — before any code is written. It maps a user's goal to one of four foundational patterns: a **Linear Chain** (relay race), a **Manager-Led Orchestration** (hub and spoke), a **Parallel Fan-Out** (concurrent independent tasks), or a **ReAct Loop** (single agent with tool access). The wrong pattern for a task creates unnecessary complexity; the right pattern makes the system maintainable and scalable.
when_to_use: Use this skill for agentic workflow design tasks.
